items.headline: Zehn Jahre nach OEZ-Anschlag: Hinterbliebene fühlen sich im Stich gelassen
items.summary: Vor zehn Jahren tötete ein rechtsextremer Attentäter neun Menschen am Olympia-Einkaufszentrum in München. Behörden werteten die Tat lange als unpolitischen Amoklauf - mit Folgen für die Betroffenen. Von T. Marsen.

items.headline: Lob für Alkoholverbot an Bahnhöfen - aber Fragen zur Umsetzung
items.summary: Für das geplante Alkoholverbot an Bahnhöfen gibt es Lob aus der Politik und von Polizeigewerkschaften. Zugleich werden Zweifel an der Umsetzung und Kritik an den Folgen für bestimmte Personengruppen laut.

items.headline: Bis zu 200 Prozent: Trump kündigt Zölle auf Arzneimittel an
items.summary: US-Präsident Donald Trump will Arzneimittelhersteller zu einer Produktion in den USA bewegen. Für Generika kündigte er deshalb ab Sommer 2028 Strafzölle an - die auf bis zu 200 Prozent steigen sollen.

items.headline: KI von OpenAI steuert eigenständig Hackerangriff
items.summary: OpenAI spricht selbst von einem "beispiellosen Cybervorfall": KI-Modelle der Firma brachen eigenständig aus einer Testumgebung aus, verschafften sich Zugang zum offenen Internet - und hackten ein externes Unternehmen.

items.headline: Hegseth beziffert Kriegskosten in Iran auf 37,5 Milliarden Dollar
items.summary: US-Verteidigungsminister Hegseth hat sich den Fragen des Senatsausschusses gestellt. Denn er will mehr Geld für den Iran-Krieg. 37,5 Milliarden Dollar hat der Konflikt die USA schon gekostet - mehr als bisher angenommen.

items.headline: Handelsroute: Nordwestpassage als neuer globaler Seeweg?
items.summary: Die Nordwestpassage galt lange als gefährlichster Seeweg der Welt. Der Klimawandel weckt Hoffnungen, dass hier eine neue Handelsroute zwischen Ostasien und Nordamerika entstehen könnte. Ein realistisches Szenario? Von M. Ganslmeier.

items.headline: Braunau in Österreich: Hitlers Geburtshaus wird zu Polizeistation
items.summary: Erst ein Schulgebäude, dann ein Standort der Lebenshilfe, zuletzt Leerstand: Lange wurde im österreichischen Braunau diskutiert, was aus dem Geburtshaus von Hitler werden soll. Nun soll die Polizei den Ort "neutralisieren". Von D. Freches.

items.headline: Londoner Polizei: Festnahmen mithilfe von Gesichtserkennung
items.summary: Die Londoner Polizei preist ihre Erfahrungen mit automatisierter Gesichtserkennung. Mehr als 2.000 Gesuchte seien mit ihrer Hilfe festgenommen worden. Nun soll das Projekt ausgerollt werden. Datenschützer sind erschrocken. Von C. Prössl.

items.headline: KI-Modelle von OpenAI steckten hinter IT-Angriff auf Hugging Face
items.summary: Bei einem Test der Fähigkeiten neuer KI-Modelle sind die aus ihrer Umgebung ausgebrochen und haben Hugging Face kompromittiert. Das hat OpenAI eingestanden.

items.headline: Nextcloud: Wirrwarr um Website-Störung – Cyberangriff bestätigt
items.summary: Am Sonntag ging die Webseite von Nextcloud vorübergehend offline. Der Anbieter bestätigt auf Anfrage einen Cyberangriff.

items.headline: Suno-Datenleck: Have I Been Pwned ergänzt 55 Millionen Konten
items.summary: Das Have-I-Been-Pwned-Projekt hat mehr als 55 Millionen Konten aus dem Suno-Datenleck zur Datenhalde hinzugefügt.

items.headline: AliExpress: EU-Kommission verhängt DSA-Rekordstrafe
items.summary: Der chinesische Onlinemarktplatz AliExpress hat über Jahre gegen den Digital Services Act verstoßen und muss nun 550 Millionen Euro zahlen.

items.headline: Microsoft arbeitet an Lösung für WSUS-Störungen
items.summary: Die Update-Software WSUS hat seit vergangener Woche mit Problemen zu kämpfen, hat Microsoft nun eingeräumt. Es gibt erste Fixes.

items.headline: WTF: Billionenbeträge berechnet: Abrechnungsfehler bei Amazon Web Services
items.summary: Kunden wurden plötzlich astronomische Kosten für Amazon Web Services angezeigt. Dahinter steckt ein technischer Fehler.

items.headline: Moonshot AI präsentiert Kimi K3: Chinas Antwort auf OpenAI und Anthropic
items.summary: Chinas KI-Start-up Moonshot AI setzt die USA mit seinem neuen Sprachmodell unter Druck. Wie reagieren Washington und das Silicon Valley auf den Wettbewerb?

items.headline: LHS 1140 b: Erstmals Atmosphäre bei erdähnlichem Exoplaneten nachgewiesen
items.summary: Seit seiner Entdeckung gilt LHS 1140 b als besonders interessant für die Suche nach außerirdischem Leben. Nun wurde bei dem Exoplaneten Helium entdeckt.

items.headline: Jugendschutzgesetz wirkungslos: Australiens Social-Media-Verbot scheitert an Teenagern
items.summary: Eine Studie zeigt: Die Befolgungsquote des Social-Media-Verbots für Jugendliche unter 16 Jahren in Australien sinkt dramatisch. (Social-Media-Verbot, Studien)

items.headline: China: Weltweit größte Hybrid-Solaranlage liefert Energie auch bei Nacht
items.summary: Damit dies funktioniert, wird mit Solarenergie Salz erhitzt, dessen Abwärme über Nacht eine Dampfmaschine antreibt. (Solarenergie, Umweltschutz)

items.headline: Anzeige: Garmin Instinct 3 Solar mit unbegrenzter Akkulaufzeit zum Bestpreis
items.summary: Die Garmin Instinct 3 Solar (45 mm) ist bei Amazon zum Bestpreis erhältlich und bietet dank Solarladung eine nahezu unbegrenzte Akkulaufzeit. (Smartwatch, GPS)

items.headline: Frankreich: Social Media wird ab September für unter 15-Jährige gesperrt
items.summary: Die Französische Nationalversammlung und der Senat haben sich auf ein Gesetz geeinigt, das Jugendlichen unter 15 Jahren den Zugang zu sozialen Netzwerken verbietet. (Soziales Netz, Politik)

items.headline: Bei internen KI-Tests: OpenAI hat versehentlich Hugging Face gehackt
items.summary: Der kürzlich bekannt gewordene Cyberangriff auf Hugging Face kam von OpenAI. Das Unternehmen hatte seine KI-Modelle bei internen Cybertests nicht im Griff. (KI, Cyberwar)

items.headline: Booking.com: Geschäftsmodell Endreinigung
items.summary: Wie Ferienhausanbieter auf Booking.com den Preis nach der Buchung durch Pflichtgebühren massiv erhöhen. Eine Recherche von Markus Feilner (Reisen, Elektroauto)

items.headline: Mattel Film: Masters of the Universe gratis bei Prime Video
items.summary: Amazon MGM Studios wechselt zum digitalen Vertrieb: Ab dem heutigen Mittwoch ist Masters of the Universe weltweit im Abo auf Amazon Prime Video verfügbar. (Prime Video, Digitalkino)

items.headline: Anzeige: Großer Balkonkraftwerks-Sale bei Amazon - und der Topseller ist schon weg
items.summary: Seit gestern befinden sich mehrere Balkonkraftwerke bei Amazon im zeitlich befristeten Angebot. Der erste Deal ist schon vegriffen. (Balkonkraftwerk, Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: More warehouses of Russian online retailer Wildberries hit by Ukraine, officials say
items.summary: Logistics hubs belonging to Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions were struck overnight on Tuesday.

items.headline: Japan temperatures soar over 40C, marking first 'cruelly hot day'
items.summary: The term was introduced in April by the weather agency to describe days when temperatures reach 40C.

items.headline: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
items.summary: Oleksandr Syrskyi is dismissed after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.headline: Rebel Wilson cleared in defamation case brought by actress in her own film
items.summary: Wilson was accused of defaming young Australian actress Charlotte MacInnes, but cleared by a Sydney court.

items.headline: Meet the women breaking world records in lumberjack sports
items.summary: The BBC finds out what is driving the sports' increasing popularity with women and what it takes to be the best.

items.headline: Beluga whales relocated from closed Canadian marine park arrive in US
items.summary: Six beluga whales have arrived in the US after being moved from a Canadian marine park that could no longer care for them.

items.headline: France passes law banning under-15s from social media
items.summary: The law will require social media platforms in France to verify all users age from January.

items.headline: Michelin-starred restaurateur faces jail for using ants as dessert garnish
items.summary: Officials say the restaurant in South Korea used some 49,000 ants in various dishes over four years.

items.headline: Tech Life
items.summary: Dogs have a great sense of smell. Now machines are being trained to sniff out odours.

items.headline: AI minister role boosted but tech department axed in Burnham shake-up
items.summary: Kanishka Narayan will attend cabinet - but no longer have the leadership of a technology secretary.

items.headline: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
items.summary: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.

items.headline: Ofcom unable to take further legal action against suicide forum
items.summary: The regulator said it had "no further legal routes available" under the Online Safety Act.

items.headline: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
items.summary: The Chinese retailer allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, says the EU.

items.headline: Tech Now
items.summary: Ammie Sekhon goes behind the scenes of WWE to see the tech that supports the show.

items.headline: China's Moonshot AI claims Kimi K3 can rival OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: The company has unveiled a massive new artificial intelligence model it says can take on top American firms.

items.headline: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
items.summary: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

items.headline: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
items.summary: The Range Rover GT will debut as an EV with a hybrid version to follow.

items.headline: On the run for 20 years, most-wanted fugitive caught hiding as a biotech exec
items.summary: Ronald Fischer, aka Richard Graydon, was arrested in New York last week.

items.headline: Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
items.summary: "Maybe that's my responsibility, to live alone with the truth."

items.headline: Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
items.summary: Taylor Farms stirred confusion on FDA test and provided a vague recall list.

items.headline: Nintendo says users voluntarily paid higher prices, have no right to tariff refunds
items.summary: Nintendo says Switch buyers got what they paid for, urges court to dismiss lawsuit.

items.headline: Let Tom Hiddleston be your guide to Pompeii's final day
items.summary: NatGeo's Pompeii: Out of Time fuses historical fact and imagination to bring city's last 24 hours to life.

items.headline: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
items.summary: A single chemical made by mole-rat queens enforces the social order.

items.headline: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
items.summary: Anthropic blocks authors from opting out of $1.5B settlement at last minute.

items.headline: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
items.summary: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an interactive enterprise training platform where employees practice workplace conversations with AI avatars that provide feedback, scoring, and analytics to help companies measure training effectiveness.

items.headline: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
items.summary: Anthropic and OpenAI's aggressive 2026 acquisition sprees set the stage for a weekend rumor.

items.headline: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
items.summary: At last, a tech company has found a way to outsource humanity's oldest pastime: using our imaginations.

items.headline: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
items.summary: OpenAI has come forward to claim responsibility for the Hugging Face breach, saying it was the result of internal testing gone awry.

items.headline: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
items.summary: Buzz is a group chat platform for the workplace that puts humans and their AI agents in the same conversation.

items.headline: AI and the rise of the universal entertainment app
items.summary: Over the past decade, streaming platforms competed by dominating individual formats like music, video, podcasts, or audiobooks. Now, as AI makes it easier to create, organize, and recommend content, those distinctions are fading, pushing companies like Spotify, Netflix, YouTube,

items.headline: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
items.summary: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

items.headline: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
items.summary: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

items.headline: Neill Blomkamp’s new zombie AI ‘film’ is just slop warmed over
items.summary: On Monday, District 9 and Gran Turismo director Neill Blomkamp unveiled his latest project: a 13-minute sci-fi short titled Nightborne that's loosely based on Peter Watts' 2014 novel Echopraxia. The short comes from Blomkamp's new AI startup / production company, Barley Studios,

items.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman. | Bloomberg via Getty Images OpenAI says its AI models mistakenly breached open-source AI platform Hugging Face during internal testing. In a blog post on Tuesday, OpenAI writes that GPT-5.6 Sol and "an even more capable pre-release model" discovered vulner

items.headline: Substack adds an AI detector to help spot blogs written by no one
items.summary: Substack will now help users determine whether what they're reading may have been written by AI. A new tool coming to the platform can scan posts, notes, replies, and comments to provide an estimate of how much text could be AI-generated or written with AI assistance, according t

items.headline: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
items.summary: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters. In an order on Monday, Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín writes that the settlement

items.headline: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
items.summary: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensiv

items.headline: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
items.summary: I was not a fan of the original Halliday glasses. The Gen 2 are better. | Image: Halliday I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see,

items.headline: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
items.summary: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New

items.headline: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none

items.headline: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity,

items.headline: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector data

items.headline: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
items.summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully

items.headline: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: mo

items.headline: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
items.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer c

items.headline: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
items.summary: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the

items.headline: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
items.summary: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also

items.headline: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising

items.headline: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
items.summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red

items.headline: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts

items.headline: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale

items.headline: OpenAI Models Escaped Containment and Hacked Hugging Face
items.summary: The cybersecurity-focused models, including GPT-5.6 Sol, broke out of a testing sandbox, exploited a zero-day, and gained access to the open internet to pull off the attack.

items.headline: This Former Intel CEO Wants to Jumpstart Moore’s Law With Light
items.summary: Pat Gelsinger wants to pave the way to ever more powerful artificial intelligence using tiny beams of light.

items.summary: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

items.headline: Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
items.summary: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Halliday’s New Smart Glasses Skip the Camera
items.summary: Halliday’s G2 glasses can listen to and summarize your workplace meetings—with no video involved.

items.headline: ‘It’s a Modern-Day Draft’: Why Stanford Students Walked Out on Sundar Pichai’s Commencement Speech
items.summary: Last month, more than a hundred Stanford students left their own graduation to protest Google’s military contracts and deals with ICE. Two organizers, Amanda Campos and Eva Jones, tell us why.

items.headline: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens
items.summary: Members of the Army received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens, and needed to limit use.

items.headline: Etsy Is In Its Flop Era, and Sellers Are Fleeing
items.summary: Once a quirky bastion of amateur vulva jewelry and pet portraits, Etsy is now deluged with mass-produced goods and AI knockoffs. Some customers don’t seem to mind.
